scope: Test that session files use semantic naming format ses_<timestamp>_<purpose>_<agent>.json

confirmed: Test FAILS with: 'expected 3 to be greater than or equal to 4'
Current session naming: ses_test_semantic.json (only 3 segments, no timestamp/purpose/agent decomposition)
Handler writes to .hivemind/sessions/ using getSessionPath() which returns {sessionId}.json
inferred: handleTextComplete passes sessionId directly to initSession() as-is
consolidated-writer.getSessionPath() does not transform the sessionId into semantic format
No timestamp, purpose, or agent segments are injected into the filename
unverified: Whether the semantic format should be applied at getSessionPath or at a higher layer
Whether existing session IDs (like ses_2de3aeec0ffebdZcPoIOYMsS4W) should be migrated

test_execution.failure_reason: Session filename has 3 segments (ses_test_semantic) but expected at least 4 (ses_<timestamp>_<purpose>_<agent>)

recommended_next_action: GREEN phase: modify consolidated-writer.getSessionPath() or handleTextComplete() to generate semantic filenames with timestamp, purpose class, and agent name
